page: Amazon Web Services c5d.9xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8124M processor with 36 vCPUs and 18 physical cores running at 3.4 GHz. The system is equipped with 72.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a 2.0 GB per core allocation. Local storage is bundled, consisting of a 900 GB NVMe SSD instance store, and the network interface supports a baseline bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Benchmark results show solid compute and memory performance. The instance achieved a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 33337.8 and a Passmark CPU score of 30111.19. Memory bandwidth tests reached 1854.85 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, and 101.84 GB/sec for large-block uncached reads. In application-specific workloads, the server demonstrated a static web serving throughput of 21.81 GB/sec and processed 31.21 million Redis SET operations per second. For software compilation using Clang, it scored 22728.0 on Geekbench.

With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance is optimized for compute-heavy tasks rather than memory-intensive databases. While it handles smaller LLM inference tasks adequately, such as prompt processing at 1358.19 tokens/sec for a 135M model, it struggles with larger models, dropping to 5.19 tokens/sec for a 70B model. The inclusion of local NVMe SSD storage provides high-speed, low-latency temporary storage, making it highly cost-efficient for workloads requiring high-speed scratch space. This configuration is well-suited for high-performance web serving, batch processing, software compilation, and distributed analytics.
description: Amazon Web Services c5d.9xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by a 36-vCPU Intel Xeon 8124M processor running at 3.4 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 72.0 GB of DDR4 memory and a bundled 900 GB NVMe SSD local instance store. The instance delivers strong compute performance, as evidenced by a Passmark CPU score of 30111.19 and a static web serving throughput of 21.81 GB/sec. However, with a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, it is less suited for memory-bound applications, and LLM inference performance drops significantly on larger models. It is highly efficient for workloads that require high-speed local storage alongside dedicated compute resources, such as batch processing, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.

tagline: A compute-optimized platform featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage for intensive processing workloads.
